steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The logical relationship is addition. Sentence 1 describes one set of anti-counterfeiting measures (weaving blue threads and muscovite into the paper). The blank needs a transition signaling that sentence 2 adds another distinct measure (sage leaf imprints) to the same defensive effort.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — That said,: signals a concession or qualification, implying sentence 2 partially walks back or limits sentence 1. The sage leaf step does not limit or qualify the paper-weaving step. ✗
  • Choice B — Specifically,: signals narrowing to a specific case of a general claim. Sentence 2 is not a specific instance of sentence 1; it is a separate, additional measure. ✗
  • Choice C — For example,: signals exemplification. The sage leaf imprints would have to be an example of weaving blue threads and muscovite into paper, which they are not — they are a distinct technique. ✗
  • Choice D — Moreover,: signals addition of a further point at the same level, showing Hartley used multiple independent anti-counterfeiting strategies, so fits. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
